markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Assemble final evidence pack: HBR matrix, commands, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, receipts, and known residual risks. This MT belongs to SecurityPortabilityValidation. Prove WP-009 behavior with negative fixtures, visual debugging, portability checks, and validator-ready evidence.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Validation must include negative fixtures for false PASS, stale state, missing Postgres, missing assets, secret leakage, partial PDF extraction, parallel write conflicts, and UI overlap.
Portability checks must reject hardcoded machine paths and authority stored only in generated projections.
scope.expected_behavior: IntegrationValidatorEvidencePack leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: IntegrationValidatorEvidencePack is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: commit 022657f6 on feat/WP-KERNEL-009 (+457, one new test file wp009_integration_validator_evidence_pack_tests.rs), zero .GOV

runtime proof: real-PG test PASS (36.22s, genuinely executed — not the SKIP path) — assembles a typed JSON evidence index (fail-closed if any claimed proof-file is missing) and PROVES at runtime: fail-closed authority resolver rejects missing/SQLite (HBR-STOP-001/CX-503R); no-SQLite source tripwire + machine-local-path rejection of C:/, D:\, /home, /tmp (HBR-STOP-002); live Loom visual-debug PG projection authority_backend=postgres_event_ledger (HBR-VIS-005); KNOWLEDGE_VALIDATION_RECORDED EventLedger receipt appended + re-read from PostgreSQL with matching payload_hash; all four hbr_focus rows durable across the round-trip
adversarial review (3 lenses; 2 initially FAILed on missing CAPTURED proof + a stray root test-results/) — harden re-ran and captured the real-PG PASS and removed the stray dir

VALIDATION CAVEAT: frontend pixel-level visual-debug screenshot lane (app/tests/visual Playwright) was NOT run (app/tests/visual is empty); this MT proves the BACKEND visual-debug projection from live PG and records the GUI-pixel half as a durable residual-risk note rather than overclaiming

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-240 requires a runtime-proven, validator-ready WP-009 evidence pack with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, named proof pointers, HBR rows proved/blocked/NA, and honest residual risks. The single committed test file (commit 022657f6) assembles a typed JSON evidence index and proves every hbr_focus claim (HBR-STOP-001/002, HBR-VIS-005, CX-503R) by executing real pre-existing product code against the Handshake-managed PostgreSQL + EventLedger, including a receipt append/re-read/hash-integrity round-trip; it delivers the MT behavior.
findings: Commit 022657f6 adds exactly one file (src/backend/handshake_core/tests/wp009_integration_validator_evidence_pack_tests.rs, +457, zero .GOV) — within allowed_paths tests/; no boundary/scope/hygiene violation.

sub_rule_1 (no deferred-live escape): grep of the proof file finds NO todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'pending MT-'/*Unavailable variants. The only early-return is a genuine PG-binaries-absent SKIP, and the commit message documents a real PASS (36.22s vs PG 127.0.0.1:5544), not the SKIP path.

sub_rule_2 (real Handshake-owned resource touch): every API the test calls is real pre-existing product code, NOT implementer-authored mocks — ControlPlaneStorageConfig::resolve (src/storage/mod.rs:186, genuinely returns StorageError::Validation for SQLite/missing DATABASE_URL, no SQLite fallback), scan_source_text (src/dependency_policy/source_tripwires.rs:222), normalize_source_relative_path (src/knowledge_ingestion/paths.rs:45), loom_visual_debug_snapshot (src/storage/postgres.rs:9288), wp009_surface_registry (src/user_manual/registry.rs:100), append_kernel_event + list_kernel_events_for_aggregate (src/storage/postgres.rs:13571), KnowledgeValidationRecorded (src/kernel/mod.rs:277/351).

Real-PG proof is genuine: tests/knowledge_pg_support.rs resolves POSTGRES_TEST_URL/DATABASE_URL else the Handshake-managed cluster (ManagedPostgres::ensure_running, port 5544), applies the full migration chain on a fresh isolated schema, and has NO SQLite/in-memory/mock fallback (returns None only when PG binaries are truly absent; otherwise panics).

CX-503R durable-authority is proven, not asserted: the test appends the assembled evidence index as a KNOWLEDGE_VALIDATION_RECORDED EventLedger receipt, re-reads it from PostgreSQL by aggregate, and asserts payload_hash + claims/residual_risks/hbr_rows_proved integrity (lines 6 of the test body).

All 8 claimed proof-pointer test files exist on disk (verified): security_portability_validation_tests.rs, knowledge_fail_closed_tests.rs, sandbox_escape_negative_tests.rs, replay_drive_tests.rs, loom_visual_debug_views_tests.rs, hbr_vis_gap_tests.rs, knowledge_parallel_write_conflict_fixture_tests.rs, loom_transclusion_tests.rs — the pack fail-closes (asserts is_file) on any missing pointer.

Residual risks are honestly recorded and durable: the frontend Playwright pixel-screenshot lane (app/tests/visual) is explicitly NOT claimed and is captured as a residual-risk note; HBR-VIS-005 is proven only at the BACKEND PG projection level — an accurate, non-overclaiming boundary, not a fake.

Aside (non-blocking, expected for winding-down governance): MT contract lifecycle.claimed_by=KERNEL_BUILDER with completed_by=null and status READY_FOR_VALIDATION — no self-cert; packet cites v02.192 anchors while current is v02.194 (paperwork drift, not a code defect).
evidence_checked: git show --stat + full diff of commit 022657f6
existence check of all 8 claimed proof-pointer test files

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
